2605.07483.title: Does Your Neural Network Extrapolate? Feature Engineering as Identifiability Bias for OOD Generalization
2605.07483.paper_summary: Successful deep neural networks discover salient features of data. We show when and why they fail to learn out-of-distribution (OOD)-relevant representations from an in-distribution (ID) training window. This requires decoupling feature learning from data-generating-process (DGP) identifiability. From a single training window, OOD extrapolation is non-identifiable: infinitely many DGPs are $\varepsilon$-observationally equivalent on the training data but diverge arbitrarily outside it, and no in-distribution criterion alone reliably breaks the tie. A structural commitment, the feature map, label map, and model class $(\varphi, ψ, \mathcal{M})$, dictates the assumed DGP and governs OOD generalization while leaving ID performance essentially unchanged. When architecture, pretraining, augmentation, input formats, or domain knowledge implicitly inject the missing commitment, the model succeeds. When it cannot infer OOD-relevant structure from ID evidence, it fails. Changing only the representation can make the same architecture, at the same in-distribution loss, differ by ${\sim}520\times$ out of distribution. When the commitment is correct and identifiable, OOD error vanishes. For example, Fourier coordinates turn periodic extrapolation into interpolation on $\mathbb{S}^1$. The same mechanism predicts outcomes in three natural-science settings (mass-action chemistry; Kepler's-third-law exoplanet prediction, $n=2{,}362$; and cross-species coding-DNA detection) and in a 264-run positional-encoding study across Transformer, Mamba, and S4D. Finally, a controlled study shows: correct features are necessary but not sufficient. The model class must express the target, and the transformed training data must cover the relevant representation space.
2605.07483.paper_summary_zh: 成功的深度神經網絡能夠發現數據的顯著特徵。<lb/>我們展示了它們何時以及為何無法從分佈內（ID）訓練窗口中學習到與分佈外（OOD）相關的表示。<lb/>這需要將特徵學習與數據生成過程（DGP）的可識別性解耦。<lb/>從單一的訓練窗口來看，OOD 外推是不可識別的：無限多個 DGP 在訓練數據上是 $\varepsilon$-觀察等價的，但在其外部卻任意發散，且沒有單一的分佈內標準能可靠地打破平局。<lb/>一種結構性承諾，即特徵映射、標籤映射和模型類 $(\varphi, ψ, \mathcal{M})$，決定了假定的 DGP 並主導 OOD 泛化，同時基本保持 ID 性能不變。<lb/>當架構、預訓練、增強、輸入格式或領域知識隱含地注入缺失的承諾時，模型會成功。<lb/>當它無法從 ID 證據中推斷出與 OOD 相關的結構時，它會失敗。<lb/>僅改變表示可以使相同的架構在相同的分佈內損失下，於分佈外的表現差異達到 ${\sim}520\times$。<lb/>當承諾正確且可識別時，OOD 錯誤消失。<lb/>例如，傅立葉坐標將周期外推轉變為在 $\mathbb{S}^1$ 上的內插。<lb/>相同的機制在三個自然科學設置中預測結果（質量作用化學；開普勒第三定律外行星預測，$n=2{,}362$；以及跨物種編碼 DNA 檢測）以及在跨 Transformer、Mamba 和 S4D 的 264 次運行的位置信息編碼研究中。<lb/>最後，一項受控研究顯示：正確的特徵是必要但不充分的。<lb/>模型類必須表達目標，且轉換後的訓練數據必須涵蓋相關的表示空間。
2605.07483.author: Leonel Aguilar et.al.
2605.07483.authors: Leonel Aguilar, Jan Nagler, Christoph Hoelscher, Nino Antulov-Fantulin
